slides.title: Theme E2E Coverage
slides.subtitle: All registered slide types rendered through one runtime theme contract.

slides.title: Balanced One Column Sample
slides.strapline: GOVERN | Medium density narrative with expected single-slide fit.
body: What controls are in place to monitor priority cybersecurity objectives?
Operating reviews include recurring discussions on control execution and ownership for target remediation items.
Governance records reference objective tracking, progress checkpoints, and leadership escalation routines.

slides.title: RS.CO Tactical Summary
slides.strapline: RESPOND | Balanced two-column content for non-split baseline.
leftBody: Update incident response triggers and ownership definitions.
Document one-hour communication sequencing and backup approvers.
rightBody: Align regulatory reporting timelines with internal escalation steps.
Maintain notification registers and periodic contract clause reviews.

slides.title: Diligence Requests and Priority Ranking
slides.strapline: Data requests are prioritized based on decision impact and near-term transaction readiness.

table.title: Priority Requests
table.heading: Request backlog
rows: Monthly customer retention cohorts by segment
Required to validate topline durability assumptions.
Contract-level pricing waterfall and discount approvals
Supports margin bridge substantiation and pricing control design.
Historical office lease schedule by location
Confirms non-core fixed cost exposure and relocation flexibility.

insights: High-priority asks directly influence valuation confidence and should be completed before confirmatory work begins.
Medium-priority asks can run in parallel with documented weekly milestones.
slides.notes: Source: management data room index and diligence tracker export.

slides.title: Function Trend Snapshot
slides.strapline: Chart and commentary remain balanced under default typography and spacing.
body: Performance improves steadily across the observed period.
Variance narrows in later quarters, indicating more stable execution.
Maintain current controls and focus on lagging segments.

chart.source: Source: stress dataset for chart rendering.

callouts.heading: Action
body: Prioritize aging high-severity items.

slides.title: Maturity Score by Function
slides.strapline: Function-level averages highlight where executive attention should be concentrated.
slides.heading: Function-level snapshot
body: Respond and Recover remain comparatively stronger across assessed artifacts when benchmarked against sampled maturity evidence.
Detect and Protect remain pressure points with lower control maturity and uneven monitoring cadence.
Prioritize monitoring governance and policy-to-practice execution with clear ownership and milestone tracking.

slides.title: Key findings (2/2) - Adjusted EBITDA bridge

analysisColumns.heading: FY21 to FY22, Adjusted EBITDA
body: Revenue growth reflected recovering transaction volumes.
Cost expansion remained controlled versus topline movement.

analysisColumns.heading: FY22 to FY23, Adjusted EBITDA
body: Processing services growth supported margin resilience.
Operating expenses increased with implementation activity.

analysisColumns.heading: FY23 to TTM Jun-24, Adjusted EBITDA
body: Revenue increased on higher transaction volume.
Margin progression remained supported by operating leverage.

slides.source: Source: Management information and audited financial statements.

slides.title: Business overview

perimeter.label: Transaction perimeter
perimeter.subLabel: Entities under FDD scope of work

overviewBody: Everlink Payment Services Inc. provides payment solutions to financial institutions across Canada.
Mobile payments and gateway services support transaction processing, settlement, and reporting.
Growth has been supported by volume expansion across core channels.
slides.source: Source: Audited financial statements and management information.

slides.title: Workstream Priorities and Initial Owners
slides.strapline: Program structure aligns initiative owners with measurable outcomes and clear sequencing.

columns.heading: Revenue
body: Prioritize retention interventions in top churn cohorts.
Establish pipeline conversion governance with weekly owner accountability.

columns.heading: Cost
body: Consolidate duplicative vendors and sequence procurement events by spend category.
Launch zero-based spend reviews for discretionary functions.

columns.heading: Risk
body: Harden controls around privileged access and reporting deadlines.
Track remediation closure evidence with escalation thresholds.

columns.heading: Execution
body: Stand up PMO rituals with clear entry and exit criteria.
Publish a fortnightly steering committee dashboard for decision support.

slides.disclaimer: This report has been prepared for discussion purposes only and should be considered together with supporting schedules and diligence workpapers provided under confidentiality restrictions.
